citations: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 2-7: work_id: MAP-R1A, branch: mapping/map-r1a-star-suffix-canonical-fix, risk_level: HIGH, execution_model: TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH, change_type: BEHAVIOUR

changes.finding: BEHAVIOUR is correct: sprint changes canonical biomarker mapping in alias_registry_service.py and analysisBiomarkerKey() — both directly affect which biomarkers enter scoring and signal evaluation (Intelligence Core, SOP §3). HIGH is mandatory: alias_registry_service.py is the canonical mapping layer whose output determines every downstream analytical operation — signal evaluation, root-cause arbitration, and narrative generation. Any change to resolve() is an Intelligence Core interaction regardless of file path (SOP §10).

changes.field: Backend implementation file content cross-referenced against prompt root cause (Stage 2A)
changes.finding: File read and root cause verified. alias_registry_service.py resolve() at line 303 has no trailing abnormal-marker stripping. _strip_surrounding_punctuation() at line 267 strips string.punctuation minus underscore — this includes '*', '†', '(', ')' — confirming the truncation bug where '*' is stripped then ')' is stripped, producing 'homocysteine_(venous' instead of 'homocysteine_(venous)'. _strip_specimen_suffix() at line 285 checks string.endswith(f'_({suffix})') — fails when string ends in '*'. All three lookup paths in resolve() (lines 321-336) therefore miss. Prompt root cause description is accurate.
citations: backend/core/canonical/alias_registry_service.py line 303: def resolve(self, name: str) -> str:
backend/core/canonical/alias_registry_service.py lines 285-301: _strip_specimen_suffix checks endswith('_(venous)') etc. — fails when * is trailing
backend/core/canonical/alias_registry_service.py lines 321-336: three lookup paths, all fail for *-suffixed keys

changes.field: Frontend implementation file content cross-referenced against prompt fix requirement (Stage 2A)
changes.finding: File read and fix target verified. analysisBiomarkerKey() at line 415 converts spaces to underscores and lowercases but does not strip trailing '*'. Defence-in-depth fix (Section B of prompt) is correctly scoped to this function only.
citations: frontend/app/lib/uploadReferenceRange.ts lines 415-422: analysisBiomarkerKey() — base = displayName.trim().toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, '_') — no * stripping

changes.finding: Sprint scope is explicitly bounded to canonical alias resolution only: strip trailing abnormal markers before alias lookup. Section C explicitly prohibits touching scoring thresholds, severity labels, unit conversion, clinical interpretation, Knowledge Bus assets, or root-cause rules. changes.field: Risk level under-classification in MAP-R1 investigation paper — non-blocking
changes.finding: MAP-R1 investigation report Section 10 recommends risk_level: STANDARD for the proposed MAP-R1A sprint. The prompt correctly elevates this to HIGH. alias_registry_service.py is the canonical mapping layer that directly gates all downstream Intelligence Core operations. Any change to resolve() is an Intelligence Core interaction (SOP §3 and §10). The investigation's STANDARD recommendation appears to have under-weighted the canonical mapping layer's role in the analytical pipeline. HIGH in the prompt is correct and more conservative.
changes.resolution: Non-blocking. The prompt risk level is correct. Cursor uses the prompt, not the investigation recommendation.

changes.field: H/L stripping risk — safe constraint documented (Stage 2B)
changes.finding: Prompt correctly flags H and L abnormal markers as ambiguous (they appear in canonical names: HDL, LDL, HbA1c). The safe implementation is to strip only '*' and '†' from the end of raw names before normalization. These characters never appear in any canonical biomarker name or alias. Implementation must NOT strip H or L. Prompt language 'only strip if clearly appended as a lab abnormal marker' is sound guidance; in practice this means '*' and '†' only.
changes.resolution: Non-blocking. Implementation constraint for Cursor: strip only ['*', '†'] as trailing abnormal markers. Do not attempt to strip H or L.

changes.field: Execution model discrepancy — investigation vs prompt — non-blocking
changes.finding: MAP-R1 investigation report Section 10 recommends execution_model: SINGLE_PHASE. The prompt uses TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH. TWO_PHASE is more conservative and is always valid for HIGH risk work (SOP §4). No action required.
changes.resolution: Non-blocking. TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH is correct for a HIGH risk sprint.
